title: Softmax
description: A function that turns a vector of logits into a probability distribution that sums to one.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: Softmax is the usual final normalization for multiclass outputs in language models. Given a logits vector z, it returns probabilities p_i proportional to exp(z_i), divided by the sum across all classes.

whyItMatters.title: Why It Matters
whyItMatters.body: Next-token sampling reads softmax probabilities. Cross-entropy training compares softmax outputs to target tokens. Any discussion of model confidence, entropy, or temperature assumes you understand this mapping from logits to a distribution.

simpleExample.title: Simple Example
simpleExample.body: With logits [2.0, 1.0, 0.0], softmax assigns the largest mass to index 0 because exp(2) dominates, but all three entries receive strictly positive probability. Dividing logits by temperature before softmax sharpens or flattens that distribution.

whereItAppears.title: Where It Appears
whereItAppears.body: Autoregressive transformers apply softmax along the vocabulary dimension of the logits tensor at each position. Frameworks expose it as nn.Softmax, jax.nn.softmax, or fused kernels inside sampling loops. Distinctive chain phrase: vocabulary softmax normalization at decode time.

commonConfusions.title: Common Confusions
commonConfusions.body: Softmax is not the same as a single activation inside a hidden layer—those may be ReLU or GELU. Softmax also differs from sigmoid, which handles binary probabilities. Applying softmax twice does not generally recover the original logits.

openingSummary: Softmax converts logits into nonnegative probabilities that sum to 1 across the vocabulary—the standard step from raw scores to interpretable chances.
